---
title: "Session 1 · Orientation & R Setup"
---

## Overview

Welcome to BER 640! This opening session sets up the computing environment we will rely on all semester and introduces the building blocks of R. No prior programming experience is assumed --- we start from a blank slate and make sure everyone can install the software, run code, and read data into R before we touch any statistics.

## Learning objectives

By the end of this session, you will be able to:

- Install and configure R and RStudio on your own machine.
- Navigate the RStudio interface and run code from both the console and a script.
- Create and manipulate R's core objects: vectors and data frames.
- Import an external dataset into R and inspect its structure.
- Recognize common R syntax and error messages well enough to begin troubleshooting.

## This session's materials

- {{< fa book-open-reader >}} **Read:** [Appendix A: R Setup Guide](/notes/appendices/a-r-setup-guide.html){target="_blank"}
- {{< fa laptop-code >}} **Lab:** [Lab 1: Introduction to R](/labs/labs/lab01-intro-to-r.html){target="_blank"}

## Key concepts

| Concept | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| **R** | A free, open-source language and environment for statistical computing and graphics. |
| **RStudio** | An integrated development environment (IDE) that makes writing and running R code easier. |
| **Object** | A named container that stores a value, created with the assignment operator `<-`. |
| **Vector** | An ordered collection of values of the same type (numeric, character, or logical). |
| **Data frame** | A rectangular table in which columns are variables and rows are observations. |
| **Function** | A reusable command, such as `mean()` or `read.csv()`, that takes inputs and returns output. |
| **Importing data** | Reading an external file (e.g., a CSV) into R so that it can be analyzed. |
